title: Warehouse Worker

rationales.taskAutomation: Robotic picking, sorting, and shelf-moving are real in modern fulfillment centers, but most physical pick-pack-load work across the broader warehouse base is still done by hand.
rationales.toolMaturity: Autonomous mobile robots and pick-assist systems (Amazon robotics, Locus, 6 River) are mature, widely-shipped products in large modern facilities.
rationales.adoption: Robotics adoption is common among large e-commerce and 3PL operators and spreading, though most of the smaller warehouse base still runs largely manual operations.
rationales.agenticExposure: Inventory tracking, slotting, and order-routing decisions are agent-ownable; the physical handling of goods is not.

tasksGrowing: Operating and troubleshooting robotic pick/pack systems
Exception handling for damaged, mislabeled, or irregular items
Quality control and safety oversight on automated floors
salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 53-7062, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: Quality control and safety inspection on automated lines

insight: Large e-commerce and 3PL fulfillment centers already run substantial robotics, but that has mostly shifted the job toward robot-adjacent work (loading, exception handling, machine oversight) rather than eliminating headcount outright. The smaller, non-automated warehouse base, still the majority of facilities, faces materially less near-term pressure.
